name: supabase
explanation: Supabase exposes two Prometheus-compatible endpoints. The hosted `privileged_metrics_endpoint` lives at `https://<project-ref>.supabase.co/customer/v1/privileged/metrics` — it requires (a) the per-project subdomain `<project-ref>` and (b) HTTP Basic Auth where the password is the service-role JWT pulled from the Supabase dashboard. Neither is discoverable on the wire. The self-hosted `storage_api_endpoint` at `http://%%host%%:5000/metrics` is unauthenticated and lives on a fixed port, but the README scopes it to self-hosted deployments and the check requires at least one of the two endpoints. The dominant production deployment of Supabase is the hosted offering, where the service-role JWT is the irreducible blocker, so classify as impossible. The storage path could be picked up by an OpenMetrics port-scan in self-hosted setups; capture that as a note.
notes: Pattern F-adjacent (dual-mode Prometheus): hosted path needs project-ref + service-role JWT; self-hosted Storage API path on fixed port 5000 is unauthenticated and would be discoverable by openmetrics-port-scan in isolation. Auth on the hosted path dominates the classification.
